summary: Acer sieboldianum ( Siebold's maple; Japanese: コハウチワカエデ, romanized: kohauchiwakaede ) is a species of maple native to Japan and common in the forests of Hokkaidō, Honshū, Shikoku and Kyūshū Islands; in the south of the range it is restricted to mountain forests. It is named after Philipp Franz von Siebold.
